calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-15-2023 Vacated

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-21-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Marriannne Taleghani, Esq. (415.926.7600; mtaleghani@kdvlaw.com) of Kaufman Dolowich & Voluck, LLP for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant.
Cory Baskin, Esq. (818.296.9508; cb@witkowlaw.com) of Withow Baskin for Defendant/Cross-Complainant.

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT IDEO, L.P.'S, MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT.

Ideo L.P's (Ideo) motion to strike is granted with 20 days leave to amend.

The court grants Ideo's unopposed request for judicial notice, which merely asks the court to take judicial notice of Ideo's First Amended Complaint and Fruit Street Health P.B.C.'s ("Fruit") Cross-Complaint. (See Evidence Code sec. 452(d) [judicial notice of court records].)

Fruit's Cross-Complaint seeks consequential damages. (Cross-Complaint, pars. 26, 41, 44, 54, prayer.) Section 7 of the parties' "Services Agreement," which is attached as exhibit A to the Cross-Complaint, bars the recovery of such damages. A limitation of liability clause is fully enforceable in California unless it is unconscionable. (See Food Safety Net Services v. Eco Safe Systems USA, Inc. (2012) 209 Cal.App.4th 1118, 1126.) Fruit has 20 days leave to amend to plead ultimate facts alleging how the limitation of liability clause is unconscionable.

Order is signed.

Judge: Curtis E Karnow, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/CEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-29-2022 Set For Trial On Oct-09-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
